title: Greece is turning its coal heartland into a green energy hub
gist: WEF video summary on Greece's Western Macedonia, a former lignite-mining region that supplied 70% of Greece's energy, where utility PPC S.A. under CEO Stassis plans to rebuild prosperity via hydro, solar, clean-energy storage and a massive data center, framed as a shift 'from coal to AI.'

insights.claim: Western Macedonia, a former Greek industrial stronghold that supplied 70% of Greece's energy through lignite mining, is being repositioned by utility PPC S.A. into a clean-energy and information-era hub featuring hydro, solar, energy storage and a massive data center.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: A concrete coal-to-clean plus data-center repurposing of legacy energy land; a template for stranded-asset transitions and a read on PPC's strategy.
insights.quote: It features hydro and solar facilities, clean energy storage, and a massive data center that aims to return Western Macedonia to prosperity and competitiveness.

insights.claim: PPC S.A. CEO Stassis frames the region's reinvention as a move 'from coal to AI,' pairing renewable generation with data-center demand.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Illustrates utilities anchoring new load growth with AI/data-center demand co-located with renewables; a structural power-demand theme.
insights.locator: Greece is turning its coal heartland into a green energy hub
